title: hieracium murorum subsp. filicladum
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. filicladum, also known as Wall Hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family Asteraceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it a popular remedy for wounds and skin conditions. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. Hieracium murorum subsp. filicladum can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
